title: Citrusy Gooey Middle-Eastern Eggs Slate-Tile Shakshuka
description: A crowd-pleasing dish featuring eggs, warm citrus flavors and gooey centers, served in a slate tile.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a 12-inch cast-iron skillet over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until lightly browned, 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the tomato and cook, stirring occasionally, until it releases its juices and begins to break down, 3-4 minutes.
Add the paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Create 4 wells in the vegetables and crack an egg into each well.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake until the whites are set and the yolks are still runny, 12-15 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the oven and stir in the lemon juice.
Sprinkle the parsley over the top and serve immediately.
Let the shakshuka rest for 2-3 minutes before serving to allow the eggs to set slightly.
Serve the shakshuka hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Refrigerate any leftovers and reheat in the microwave or oven until warm and fragrant.
